assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 15: 'Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN — eval-3 has 2 failing assertions at 85% pass rate (convention upgrade eligibility not evaluated for comment 30002, no sub-task created for comment 30002); overall eval pass rate 91%'. The Test Quality verdict is explicitly WARN, driven by Eval Quality WARN from eval-3's 2 failing assertions.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: Report lines 29-34: 'Review ID 40001 matched all three eval result detection criteria: 1. Author is github-actions[bot] — YES, 2. Body contains ## Eval Results — YES, 3. Body contains footer pattern sdlc-workflow/run-evals — YES'. The Test Quality row (line 15) reflects extracted eval metrics: 85% pass rate for eval-3, 91% overall, and 2 failing assertions.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md line 7: 'Type: Inline review comment (NOT an eval result)'. Lines 13-18 apply the 3-criteria heuristic with all three failing (author is reviewer-b not github-actions[bot], no '## Eval Results', no footer). The comment is classified as 'Code Change Request' through normal classification pipeline. No eval metrics appear in the classification reasoning.

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 15: 'Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN — eval-3 has 2 failing assertions at 85% pass rate (convention upgrade eligibility not evaluated for comment 30002, no sub-task created for comment 30002); overall eval pass rate 91%'. Both failing assertion topics (convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation) are mentioned in the Test Quality row.

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md: 'Type: Eval failure sub-task', titled 'Fix eval-3 assertion failures — convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation'. Lines 40-53 contain the Review Context section with both failing assertion texts and their evidence verbatim.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md lines 40-53: '## Review Context' section quotes both assertions: (1) 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002...' with evidence 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility...', and (2) 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path...' with evidence 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002...'. Both assertion texts and evidence texts are fully quoted.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md lines 48-49: '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. subtask-2.md lines 43-44: identical '## Target PR' section with the same URL.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 8: 'Root-Cause Investigation | DONE | Eval-3 failures traced to implement-task phase — convention upgrade eligibility not evaluated for suggestions; reviewer feedback traced to convention gap (Markdown exclusion not addressed)'. The verdict is 'DONE', not 'N/A'. Lines 67-77 provide detailed root-cause analysis for eval-3 failures including defect identification, universality test, method-vs-fact test, and phase attribution.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only: report.md, review-50001.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md, subtask-1.md, and subtask-2.md. All are verification artifacts (report, review classification, criterion evaluations, sub-task descriptions). No source code files (.ts, .js, .py, .rs, .go, etc.) or skill definition files (style-conventions.md, SKILL.md) appear in the outputs, confirming no code files were modified.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: Report line 9: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR modifies exactly the 2 files specified in the task: style-conventions.md and SKILL.md' — compares diff files against task's Files to Modify. Line 12: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No sensitive patterns detected in added lines' — scans diff for secrets. Line 14: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 7/7 criteria met — Check 6 scans for new symbols, verifies doc comments per language convention, produces correct PASS/WARN/N/A verdicts, output format includes sixth row, and Step 6a verdict mapping includes Documentation Coverage' — evaluates each criterion against diff content. None of these reference eval results or metrics.
